result.status_detail: Cycle 94 R-12 notion_ndjson v3 verify (single-tool-call envelope: toolName=compose_response, state=applied, result.output=warning text, result.headerLabel='보안 경고'). Result: thread URL changes (/chat?t={hex_token} new thread) but page renders COMPLETELY EMPTY — no user bubble, no answer text, no Thinking indicator. Tool-call envelope did not trigger UI render. Same effective state as cycle 93 #570 (silent_hang) but with newer architecture data.
result.experiment: Experiment v3: single-tool-call NDJSON envelope (toolName=compose_response, state=applied, result.output=warning text + result.headerLabel='보안 경고')
result.verdict: URL routing works but UI render fails. Single-tool-call structure does not match Notion's expected agent execution pattern. Likely missing fields/calls.
next_experiment_recommendations: v4: Capture COMPLETE NDJSON response body of #590 benign run (full sequence, not just preview snippet) — multiple tool-calls likely needed (e.g., search/fetch tools BEFORE compose_response)
v5: Add traceId, startedAt, finishedAt timestamps matching the agent log structure
v6: Match the exact moduleInfo schema (id, name='fs-mo...', type='fs') seen in #590 — toolName 'compose_response' may not be a real Notion tool name; need actual final-step tool name
v7: Check if response should NOT be zstd-compressed — APF may be sending uncompressed but client expects matching Content-Encoding header
v8: Inspect if a 'finalize' or 'completion' tool-call is the actual answer-rendering trigger in the full benign capture
result.regression_baseline: #570 cycle 93 (silent_hang for sensitive prompt), #590 cycle 94 (tool-call architecture identified)

notes: Cycle 94 R-12 notion v3: routing OK (new thread URL), render FAIL. Single tool-call insufficient. Need full multi-tool-call sequence + correct moduleInfo + content-encoding handling. Recommend HAR export of #590 successful run for complete schema reverse-engineering.
